body:
**Preferred term label:**
occlusal surface of tooth

**Synonyms**
chewing surface, masticatory surface

**Definition (free text, please give PubMed ID)**
A tooth surface structure that forms the chewing edge of premolar or molar tooth. It functions chew or grind food during biting.
https://dentaleducationhub.com/surfaces-of-the-teeth/
https://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem-FDI-surface.html

**Parent term (use https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/uberon)**
tooth surface structure <http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_8600148>

**Your nano-attribution (ORCID)**
https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9625-1899
https://orcid.org/0009-0002-7282-0836

cc @finn1928

@aleixpuigb sorry we forgot to include this one as well

comments.body: Ok, I will add and make an Uberon release, there hasn't been one in a long time. Thanks!

title: NTR: occlusal surface of tooth
